title: ajuga vulgaris var. breviproles
intro: Ajuga vulgaris var. breviproles, also known as Bugleweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its sedative and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety, as well as alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism. The plant's health benefits include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic, making it a useful remedy for various health conditions. Bugleweed is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be used in herbal infusions and topical applications to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
